interactions.description: Paracetamol (Crocin) may increase the blood-thinning effect of warfarin and raise bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine without your doctor's advice. Watch for unusual bruising or bleeding.

interactions.description: Pantoprazole (Pan-D) can reduce methotrexate clearance and increase side effects.
interactions.recommendation: Tell your doctor if you take both. They may adjust timing or dose.

interactions.description: Telmisartan (Telma) with potassium supplements may cause dangerously high potassium levels.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium supplements unless prescribed. Call your doctor if you feel weak or have irregular heartbeat.

interactions.description: Alcohol with metformin increases the risk of low blood sugar and lactic acidosis.
interactions.recommendation: Limit alcohol and discuss safe limits with your doctor.

interactions.description: Aspirin with warfarin greatly increases bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: This combination is usually avoided. Call your doctor immediately.

effects: Nausea or stomach upset in some people
Liver harm if taken above the prescribed dose
Rare skin rash or allergic reaction
Long-term use may affect vitamin B12 absorption
Dizziness, especially when standing up quickly
Rare swelling of face or lips
Nausea, bloating, or loose stools when starting
Rare lactic acidosis — seek help for severe weakness or breathing trouble
